summary: Researcher specializing in mass spectrometry-based proteomics, currently at the University of Washington studying protein modification networks, glycoproteomics, and cell heterogeneity in cancer. Previously completed doctoral research at the University of Florida investigating plant immune signaling through phosphoproteomics and kinase-substrate interaction mapping.
skills: Quantitative & multiplexed proteomics (TMT, spectral library matching)
Protein-protein interaction mapping (co-IP, TurboID proximity labeling)

bullets: Mass spectrometry method development for quantitative and multiplexed proteomics
Collaborative proteomics on cancer cell heterogeneity and glycoprotein interaction studies

experience.org: University of Florida — Sixue Chen Lab
bullets: Studied MPK4 kinase signaling networks in plant immunity using phosphoproteomics
Mapped kinase-substrate interactomes via TurboID proximity labeling and co-immunoprecipitation
